description: You are a medical scientist verifying the sequence of a published hemoglobin crystal structure against reference sequence databases in UGENE. Extract the amino acid sequence of a beta chain (Chain B or D) from the hemoglobin PDB structure at ~/UGENE_Data/hemoglobin_4HHB.pdb and save it to ~/UGENE_Data/pdb_cross_alignment/pdb_beta_chain.fasta. Combine this extracted sequence with the 8 reference sequences from ~/UGENE_Data/hemoglobin_beta_multispecies.fasta into a single file containing all 9 sequences at ~/UGENE_Data/pdb_cross_alignment/combined_sequences.fasta. Perform a Multiple Sequence Alignment (MSA) using MUSCLE on this combined set and export the results in ClustalW format to ~/UGENE_Data/pdb_cross_alignment/cross_species_alignment.aln and in Aligned FASTA format to ~/UGENE_Data/pdb_cross_alignment/cross_species_alignment.fasta. Finally, create a summary report at ~/UGENE_Data/pdb_cross_alignment/verification_report.txt that includes the total number of sequences in the alignment, the exact residue length of the extracted PDB beta chain, an assessment of whether the extracted sequence matches the human reference sequence (P68871), and a list of at least 4 species included in the alignment.

metadata.data_source_fasta: UniProt hemoglobin beta subunit (8 diverse species including Human P68871)
